skill_card.name: .NET Version Migration Orchestrator Agent
skill_card.description: A conductor agent that orchestrates a 7-step .NET version migration pipeline (e.g. net6.0 → net8.0, or .NET Framework → .NET Core/Modern). It auto-detects the migration mode (Single-File, Multi-File, or Full Project) and runs only the applicable subagents. It does not analyze, refactor, build, or test code itself; it invokes the Codebase Analysis, Dependency Mapping, API Compatibility, Code Refactoring, Build & Compilation, Test Execution, Migration Critique, and Reporting subagents, each writing JSON artifacts into a per-repo migrated-output/{repoName}/.migration/ folder. It enforces a strict golden rule across every subagent — the original source project is never read-write, only read-only — so a failed migration carries zero risk to the source. On a build failure it first runs an iterative build→fix loop (feeding MSBuild errors back to the Code Refactoring Agent, up to maxBuildFixIterations); only if the build is still red does it act on rollbackOnFailure — the default (false) preserves migrated-output/{repoName}/ and still generates the report so the developer keeps an actionable artifact, while the explicit opt-in (true) invokes the Rollback Agent to delete migrated-output/{repoName}/ and halts. On success it proceeds through testing and the Migration Critique Agent before final report generation. It delivers an auditable, source-safe .NET modernization workflow with isolated per-repo outputs and a final human-readable migration-report.md.

tasks.name: Initialize repo-scoped run and discover solution structure
tasks.description: Derives {repoName} from the source project root folder, initializes migrated-output/{repoName}/ with a full unmodified copy of the source (creating it if absent, leaving any other repo's output folder untouched), then invokes the Codebase Analysis Agent to recursively scan all .sln/.csproj/.fsproj/.vbproj files, classify project types and migration readiness, build the project dependency graph, and write solution-map.json. Never modifies the original source project.
tasks.input_schema: Source project root path, sourceVersion, targetVersion.
tasks.output_schema: migrated-output/{repoName}/ initialized with a full source copy, plus migrated-output/{repoName}/.migration/solution-map.json.

tasks.name: Invoke Dependency Mapping and API Compatibility agents
tasks.description: Calls the Dependency Mapping Agent to extract every NuGet PackageReference from the original source, resolve compatible versions for targetVersion via the NuGet API (falling back to an offline matrix if unreachable), and flag inbox/removed/no-compatible-version packages. Then calls the API Compatibility Agent to scan all original .cs files against the cumulative breaking-changes rule set for the sourceVersion→targetVersion path (e.g. BinaryFormatter removal, Thread.Abort(), Startup.cs hosting model, ConfigurationManager), tagging each issue with severity, autoFixable status, and an outputFile path inside migrated-output/.

tasks.name: Invoke Code Refactoring Agent against migrated-output/ only
tasks.description: Calls the Code Refactoring Agent to apply every auto-fixable issue from compatibility-report.json and every package/TFM update from dependency-report.json exclusively to the copies inside migrated-output/{repoName}/ — updating .csproj TargetFramework, PackageReference versions, global.json SDK version, and rewriting flagged C# patterns. Inserts // TODO [MIGRATION] markers for non-auto-fixable issues, validates every modified file with Roslyn, and restores any file from the original source if a transformation breaks syntax.
tasks.output_schema: Modified .csproj/.cs files inside migrated-output/{repoName}/ and refactoring-summary.json.

tasks.name: Build and gate the pipeline; build→fix loop, preserve-or-rollback on failure
tasks.description: Calls the Build & Compilation Agent to run dotnet restore and dotnet build --configuration Release exclusively against migrated-output/{repoName}/, classify every MSBuild error/warning, and write build-result.json. On a build failure it does NOT roll back immediately: it runs an iterative build→fix loop, handing the classified, deduped error set to the Code Refactoring Agent and rebuilding incrementally up to maxBuildFixIterations (default 6), then confirms with a final --no-incremental build. Only if still red does it honor rollbackOnFailure — default false preserves migrated-output/{repoName}/ and continues to Reporting with the remaining errors/TODOs; explicit true invokes the Rollback Agent, which deletes only migrated-output/{repoName}/ (other repo folders and the original source untouched) and halts. On success it proceeds to Test Execution.
tasks.input_schema: refactoring-summary.json, targetVersion, installed .NET SDK, rollbackOnFailure/maxBuildFixIterations config.
tasks.output_schema: build-result.json with outcome Success/Failed (plus buildIterationsToGreen); on failure either preserved output + report (default) or a Rollback Complete report and pipeline halt.

tasks.name: Run tests, critique quality, and generate the sign-off report
tasks.description: Gated on build success, calls the Test Execution Agent to run dotnet test against migrated-output/{repoName}/, classifying every failure as MigrationCaused, PreExisting, or Incompatible without ever triggering rollback on test failures. Optionally invokes the Migration Critique Agent to score the run across build integrity, test results, dependency health, modernization, TODO debt, and reversibility, producing a letter grade and shipping-readiness verdict. Finally invokes the Reporting Agent to aggregate every upstream JSON artifact into a single migrated-output/{repoName}/migration-report.md with TODO markers, manual-action items, and next steps.
tasks.input_schema: build-result.json (must be Success), solution-map.json, dependency-report.json, compatibility-report.json, refactoring-summary.json.
